tasks.name: A/B winner picker implementation

key_decisions: Default metric is open rate; configurable via abTestConfig.metric
Skip remainder send if 100% in test cohort
risks: Need to inspect abTestConfig cohort percentage encoding before remainder send can compute correctly
tasks.acceptance_criteria: After both variants sent + test window elapses, one variant marked winner with abWinnerPickedAt set. Remainder cohort receives winner within 15min.
tasks.completion_notes: A/B winner picker shipped. Two-proportion Z-test over open rates; |z| ≥ 1.96 (p<0.05 two-tailed) OR 48h timeout triggers pick. Three new functions in convex/email.ts: _findAbCandidates (internalQuery — scans status='sent' blasts, filters isAbTest+!abWinnerPickedAt, bounded by .take(500)); _markAbWinner (internalMutation — patches abWinnerPickedAt+updatedAt on all siblings in the group, idempotent); pickAbWinners (internalAction — wired to existing 15-min cron, groups by abParentId, computes pooled SE + z-statistic, picks higher open rate as winner). Remainder-cohort send documented as next-step — schema today doesn't capture 'remainder' as a distinct cohort vs the test cohort; operators can manually trigger a follow-up blast with the winning bodyHtml. Multi-variant (>2 siblings) deferred — first-two-only for now.

New convex/networks.getStats query iterates active members + sums supporter counts (unique by globalEmailHash), aggregates campaignActions by_orgId_verified index, deduplicates districts on districtHash, computes the same packet scalars as the per-org packet (GDS = 1-HHI over district share, ALD = unique-messages / total-with-messageHash, temporalEntropy = Shannon over hourly bins, CAI = (tier3+tier4)/max(tier1,1)). sub_steps.name: Schema: orgWebhooks + orgWebhookDeliveries + orgEvents tables

sub_steps.name: Convex dispatch internalAction with HMAC + dual-secret rotation
sub_steps.notes: convex/orgWebhooks.ts created (~290 LOC). queueEvent internalMutation fans out to orgEvents + matching orgWebhooks; deliverWebhook internalAction signs with HMAC-SHA256 + dual-rotation via Web Crypto subtle; markDelivery{Success,Dead,Retryable} mutations close the loop. Headers: X-Commons-Signature-256: t={timestamp},v1={hex_hmac}; X-Commons-Event; X-Commons-Delivery; X-Commons-Attempt. AbortSignal.timeout(10s) bounds receiver. 4xx = permanent dead; 5xx/network = retry with 2^attempt*60s backoff. Auto-disable parent after 5 consecutive dead deliveries.

sub_steps.name: Retry policy + dead-letter cron
sub_steps.notes: retryPendingDeliveries internalAction (every 1 min cron) scans by_nextRetryAt for due deliveries, schedules deliverWebhook batched at 50/tick. expireOldEvents internalMutation (hourly :47 cron) purges orgEvents older than 7 days at 1000/tick. Dead-letter handling is already in markDeliveryDead (auto-disable parent + increment failureCount). Cron entries 24 and 25 added to convex/crons.ts.

sub_steps.name: Event emission at call sites (campaigns, supporters, donations, events)
sub_steps.notes: Four queueEvent emit sites wired: convex/campaigns.ts:createCampaignAction (campaign_action.created — captures actionId, guards on orgId for defensive null-safety), convex/webhooks.ts:completeDonation (donation.completed — no PII in payload), convex/supporters.ts:create (supporter.created — no PII), convex/events.ts:createRsvp (event.rsvp_created — uses event.orgId not stringified local). Throttle for high-frequency campaign_action.created deferred — emission is per-action; downstream consumers (retry batching, SSE polling) bound the load.

Routes: /api/v1/webhooks (GET+POST), /api/v1/webhooks/[id] (GET+PATCH+DELETE), /api/v1/webhooks/[id]/rotate-secret (POST). signingSecret returned ONCE on create+rotate; never on list/get. sub_steps.notes: TS SDK: types.ts adds Webhook/WebhookCreated/WebhookSecretRotated/CreateWebhookInput/UpdateWebhookInput/WebhookEvent. index.ts adds WebhookResource with list/get/create/update/rotateSecret/delete + standalone verifyWebhookSignature using Web Crypto subtle (HMAC-SHA256, dual-secret tolerant, constant-time hex compare, 5min default skew). Python SDK: types.py mirrored. client.py: WebhookResource + AsyncWebhookResource + verify_webhook_signature using hmac/hashlib (hmac.compare_digest). __init__.py exports added. Both Commons + AsyncCommons wire .webhooks. tsc --lib ES2022,DOM clean; python3 import smoke-test green.

sub_steps.name: OpenAPI spec + integration tests
sub_steps.notes: OpenAPI spec: 4 paths (/webhooks GET+POST, /webhooks/{id} GET+PATCH+DELETE, /webhooks/{id}/rotate-secret POST) + 6 schemas (WebhookEvent enum, Webhook, WebhookCreated, WebhookSecretRotated, CreateWebhookInput, UpdateWebhookInput). Tests: tests/unit/sdk/webhook-signing.test.ts — 6 cross-verify tests covering fresh-sign roundtrip, wrong-secret rejection, tampered-payload rejection, freshness window enforcement, previous-secret acceptance during rotation, malformed-header robustness. All 6 pass. No test-fire endpoint shipped this cycle (discovery: deferred — could be added later as a one-shot dev tool but not launch-blocking).

tasks.completion_notes: Chokepoint resolved. Substrate: 3 tables (orgWebhooks/orgWebhookDeliveries/orgEvents), dispatch internalAction with HMAC-SHA256 dual-secret rotation, 2^attempt*60s retry with 5-attempt dead cutoff, 1min retry cron + hourly event-expiry cron. Surfaces: v1 CRUD (internal-secret auth) + session-auth CRUD for settings UI + settings UI at /org/[slug]/settings/webhooks. SDKs: TS + Python both wired with resources + signature-verify helpers. Spec: OpenAPI 3.1 paths+schemas published. Tests: 6 cross-verify signing tests. Production-ready for Day 1 — unblocks W-3 (T1-4 zapier-pieces, T9-7 SSE event stream, T9-8 dashboards). Discovery: T9-3.6 sub-task added a parallel session-auth surface — kept v1 internal-secret API + session-auth Convex calls separate so org settings UI doesn't need to mint API keys.

tasks.name: atlasVersion per campaignAction
tasks.description: Add atlasVersion field to campaignActions schema. Propagate from /api/submissions/create (from publicInputs or groundCredentials.atlasVersion). computeVerificationPacketCached produces driftCount + driftPct.
risks: Null atlasVersion on pre-addition rows — treat as unknown drift
tasks.acceptance_criteria: Campaign action submitted after atlas rotation stores pre-rotation atlasVersion. Packet returns non-zero driftPct.
tasks.completion_notes: atlasVersion substrate shipped end-to-end through the packet computation. Schema: campaignActions.atlasVersion: v.optional(v.string()). createCampaignAction accepts it. getActionsForPacket returns it. RawAction interface in src/lib/server/verification-packet.ts adds atlasVersion?: string | null. computePacket calls new computeAtlasDrift(actions, total) — picks the modal version as 'current' (pure computation, no env read), returns {driftCount, driftPct}. driftCount/driftPct exposed on VerificationPacket interface, null when no atlasVersion signal present so consumers can distinguish 'no drift' from 'no data'. emptyPacket includes the null defaults. Two test fixtures (org landing mock + email-report unit test) updated. Discovery: actual propagation from /api/submissions/create through to the createCampaignAction call site is in convex/submissions.ts and adjacent — substrate is in place, but threading atlasVersion through requires picking up groundCredentials.atlasVersion or publicInputs[?] in those bridges. Documented as a follow-up at the writer-side without expanding T10-9 scope.
